# pi-lcm

> Lossless context management for Pi. Replaces lossy compaction with a DAG-based summarization system backed by SQLite.

## What It Does

Pi's default compaction discards old messages permanently. pi-lcm stores every message in SQLite and creates hierarchical summaries (a DAG) when compaction triggers. The agent can search and drill into any compressed node to recover original detail.

## Key Capabilities

- Permanent message persistence in SQLite with FTS5 search
- Hierarchical DAG summarization (leaf D0 → condensed D1 → D2+)
- Three agent tools: lcm_grep (search), lcm_describe (overview), lcm_expand (drill-down)
- Parallel compaction with per-conversation mutex
- Prompt-cache-safe system prompt injection (static const)
- Interactive TUI settings panel (/lcm-settings)
- Cross-session history within the same project

## Usage

```bash
pi install npm:pi-lcm
```

Works automatically after install. Agent tools appear when history has been compacted.

## File Index

- [README.md](README.md): Full documentation, configuration, commands
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): Development setup
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): Version history
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting
- [PLAN.md](PLAN.md): Architecture design and audit history
- [index.ts](index.ts): Extension entry point
- [src/db/](src/db/): SQLite connection, schema, data access
- [src/compaction/](src/compaction/): DAG compaction engine, prompts, assembler
- [src/tools/](src/tools/): Agent tools (grep, describe, expand)
- [src/settings.ts](src/settings.ts): Settings persistence
- [src/settings-panel.ts](src/settings-panel.ts): TUI overlay
